description: Major crisis events that trigger at specific turns. Each crisis tests the institutions built in the preceding turns.

crises.title: The Grain Rot Crisis
crises.description: The granaries, built cheaply in the early years, have failed. A third of the winter stores are rotten. Thorvald demands an emergency tithe. Sigrid demands compensation by the ledger. Hrolfr demands an investigation before either can act. The Bay faces hunger by spring.

choices.text: Thorvald prevails — emergency tithe, rebuild, compensate later.
Sigrid prevails — compensate first, rebuild, tithe only if needed.
Hrolfr takes emergency command — distribute grain, rebuild, return authority.

crises.title: The Foreign Fleet Crisis
crises.description: A war fleet from a rival port city anchors off the Bay. They claim a trade dispute. They demand tribute. The harbor watch is outnumbered. The Assembly must decide: negotiate, fight, or call for a Dictator.

crises.title: The Plague Year
crises.description: Sickness spreads through the harbor district. Trade stops. The Assembly is decimated. The Tidsceal declares a year of mourning. Who governs when the governors are dying?

choices.text: The Imperator rules by decree until the plague passes.
The Assembly meets in smaller numbers. No special powers.

crises.title: The Tessera Collapse
crises.description: A fire destroys the Scriptorium's central archive. Tessera records for thousands of foreign-born residents are lost. No one can prove their status. The classification system is broken.

choices.text: Presumptive citizenship. All residents granted provisional status.
Re-register everyone. Immigration frozen until resolved.
The Court rules on each case individually.

crises.title: The Harbor Secession Crisis
crises.description: The Southern Guilds, four generations in the Bay, petition for political autonomy — a self-governing Free Port within the Bay. They control 40% of trade. Their children are citizens. This is not a threat. It's a negotiation.

choices.text: Reject. The Bay is one realm, indivisible.
